# 7.14.2.3 — Loading history: lazy fenced blocks + buffer lifecycle

**Status:** proposed

**🚫 Do not edit repository code for this plan** — assistants and automation must **not** change Vala or any other source files while iterating on this document. **This plan file only**, until the user **explicitly** asks for an implementation. Same rule is repeated in § User requirements, § 👎, § Concrete, and § Verification.

  - Emoji prefixes for discussion

- Discussion style / provenance

**Relevant checklist (topics):** (**💩** — subset from `CODING_STANDARDS.md` for this plan)

- **StringBuilder usage** — avoid `GLib.StringBuilder` unless building in loops with hundreds of iterations.
- **Building strings in loops** — follow § Building strings in loops in `CODING_STANDARDS.md`.
- **Nullable types** — prefer flags (`has_widgets`, `has_content`) over nullable widgets as the primary contract (§ Proposed behaviour).
- **`this.` prefix** — use `this.` on instance members in new or modified Vala.
- **New methods** — one approved here — `body_widgets()` (§ Outline); no others unless this plan is updated.

## Outline (read this first)

**🚫** Still **no source edits** — read the tree for facts; change **only** this plan unless implementation is explicitly requested (see banner under **Status**).

**💩** Narrative and structure below
- Assistant draft except where a line is only ⚠️ / 👎 / ℹ️ tree fact
- If the fact is from code, framing around it may still need **💩** review

**⚠️** Contract summary — no code in this plan yet (§ Concrete).

### `MarkdownGtk.Render` — **`public bool is_streaming`** (set **after** construction, not ctor arg)

- **⚠️** **`Render(Gtk.Box box)`** stays **single-argument** — **no** **`is_streaming`** in the **`Render`** ctor. Feed mode is **`public bool is_streaming { get; set; }`** on **`Render`**, set **after** **`new MarkdownGtk.Render`** by **`ChatView`**, **`oc-test-gtkmd`**, or other tests for the **top-level** renderer (§ Concrete **§ 1a.2**, **1a.3**, **1b**). **Nested** `MarkdownGtk.Render` inside a fence — **`is_streaming`** from **`has_content`** only (§ **State flags** — content routing implies widgets; § **Proposed behaviour**, § Concrete **1c**) — **not** the same assignment path as the outer **`Render`**.
- **⚠️** **`ChatView`** / tests assign **`renderer.is_streaming`** when the **feed mode** changes between assistant messages (history replay vs live stream on the **same** long-lived `Render`) — not from a GObject **`default =`** on the property (§ Concrete).
- **⚠️** **Semantics** — **`is_streaming`** is a **behaviour / feed-mode marker** (non-streaming is the usual “bulk” shape, streaming is incremental feed). It is **not** “widget state” to wipe when clearing the view.
- **⚠️** **`Render.on_code_block(true, …)`** reads **`this.is_streaming`** and passes it into **`new RenderSourceView(this, lang, this.is_streaming)`** — snapshot **at fence creation** (§ below).
- **💩** **Assistant draft (rejected)** — resetting **`this.is_streaming = false`** inside **`Render.clear()`**. **Do not.** **`clear()`** clears presentation / parser handlers; it does **not** logically discard **how the next** feed will be delivered. The marker stays meaningful across **`clear()`** unless the **app** changes it for the next message.

### How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode (fundamental)

**⚠️** **Single rule — read once.** **`RenderSourceView` has no `is_streaming` property, no `streaming` property, and no `Message` / `Message.is_stream`.** Those belong on **`MarkdownGtk.Render`** / **`Message`** only. RSV’s only feed-mode input is the **third ctor argument** (`bool`; parameter and matching **private** field names are **not** fixed in this plan — choose at implementation, § Concrete) copied from **`Render.is_streaming`** at **`new RenderSourceView`** time — **not** a public GObject property.

This was easy to bury under `Message.is_stream` prose; the rest belongs **here** in plain terms.

- **ℹ️** RSV **never** reads `OLLMchat.Message` or **`Message.is_stream`** — `libocmarkdowngtk` stays `OLLMchat`-free.
- **⚠️** **Mechanism:** the **only** `new RenderSourceView` site is **`Render.on_code_block`** (tree). **`Render`** passes **`new RenderSourceView(this, lang, this.is_streaming)`** — RSV ctor **parameter** receives the snapshot; store it in a **private** field (names § Concrete). **Do not** introduce **`this.streaming`** as if RSV had a “streaming property” — it does **not**.
- **⚠️** **`Render.is_streaming == true`** when the fence opens → third ctor arg **`true`**. **`false`** → **`false`**. Policy is fixed **at allocation**, not inferred later inside RSV.
- **ℹ️** **`Message.is_stream`** matters **above** `Render` only (**`ChatView`** → **`renderer.is_streaming`**).

- **ℹ️** **Nested `MarkdownGtk.Render`** — **`new MarkdownGtk.Render(this.rendered_box)`** then **`nested_markdown_render.is_streaming = has_content`** (§ **State flags** — `has_content` already implies embedded widgets for that routing). § Concrete **1c**. **Not** **`this.renderer.is_streaming`** and **not** the RSV ctor snapshot — those address the **fence** / **`RenderSourceView`** contract, not nested markdown routing.

- **Problem**
  - **ℹ️** History / restore replay large assistant bodies through `MarkdownGtk.Render` → `RenderSourceView`.
  - **ℹ️** Today the frame eagerly builds GtkSource + nested `MarkdownGtk.Render`.
  - **ℹ️** `add_code_text` fills buffers even when the body is hidden.

- **How fences get created (fundamental)**
  - **⚠️** `MarkdownGtk.Render` consumes markdown; when the **parser** sees an opening fence during `add()`, it calls `on_code_block(true, lang)` and **`new RenderSourceView(this, lang, this.is_streaming)`** runs there — third argument is **`Render.is_streaming`** at that instant (snapshot into RSV; § Outline **`Render.is_streaming`**).
  - **⚠️** **Live streaming feed** — set **`Render.is_streaming = true`** before **`add()`** for that message (typically via **`ChatView`** from **`Message.is_stream`**).
  - **⚠️** **History / load / restore / complete-message append** — set **`Render.is_streaming = false`** before **`add()`** for that message so lazy policy applies for bulk-shaped feeds.
  - **⚠️** **Implication:** **Do not** plumb `Message` into RSV — **`ChatView`** sets **`renderer.is_streaming`**; **`Render`** passes **`this.is_streaming`** into the RSV ctor at allocation (§ User requirements).

- **Fence delivery** (`ChatView` / `Render` only — § Wiring)
  - **ℹ️** `Message.is_stream` — on **`Message`**; **`ChatView`** maps it to **`renderer.is_streaming`** before **`add()`** for that assistant message.
  - **ℹ️** Stays on `Message` — RSV does not reference `Message`.
  - **ℹ️** **`Render.is_streaming`** → RSV **ctor third arg only** — § **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode** (do not restate “no streaming property” here).
  - **ℹ️** Incremental at this fence — many `add_code_text` before `end_code_block`.
  - **ℹ️** Single-chunk / history / restore — one shot or replay — even if the message was streamed elsewhere.

- **Bulk / single-chunk fence**
  - **⚠️** Accumulate stored body with each `add_code_text` (today `code_content` shape — § Close in § Proposed behaviour).

- **Incremental fence** (many `add_code_text`)
  - **⚠️** Render types — `.collapsed-on-done` and Open only — `.collapsed` not used for this path (§ Proposed behaviour).
  - **⚠️** While fence open — buffers hold live text when `has_widgets` — finalize `code_text` at `end_code_block` — § Proposed behaviour.
  - **👎** No unconditional `+=` into stored body on every `add_code_text` (§ 👎).

- **State flags** (`RenderSourceView` — § Proposed behaviour)
  - **⚠️** **Authoritative.** The **only** approved **`RenderSourceView` state flags** are **`has_widgets`** and **`has_content`**. Do **not** add another state flag, rename these into a larger “flag set,” or suggest extra `has_*` / parallel booleans for the same roles. Feed-mode ctor snapshots, fence chrome, `code_text`, and similar are **not** state flags here — they are covered elsewhere (§ **Lazy body**, § **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode**).
  - `has_widgets`
    - Embedded body exists (GtkSource + stack + nested markdown when applicable)
    - Set `true` only when `body_widgets()` has finished
  - `has_content`
    - Policy flag
    - When `true`, incoming `add_code_text` writes into GtkSource / nested render (requires `has_widgets`)
    - When `false`, incoming does not route to widgets
    - Not buffer length
    - Not empty vs non-empty
  - **ℹ️** Which property for which judgment — two roles, not interchangeable:
    - `has_widgets = false`
      - No embedded body yet (structural)
      - Indicator for “defer `body_widgets()`” / “nothing to append into GtkSource yet”
      - Not `source_view == null` alone (§ Acceptance)
      - Handlers: `!has_widgets` → may need `body_widgets()` first where policy allows (§ Lazy body)
    - `has_content`
      - Routing only
      - Whether incoming `add_code_text` should target widgets vs accumulate elsewhere
      - Example: `+=` stored body when buffers off-tree
      - Does not mean “embed exists”
      - Buffer writes still require `has_widgets`
      - Often one `add_code_text` while `has_widgets` false
      - Defer `body_widgets()` until policy is known
      - Then `has_content` tells whether chunk went to stored string vs buffers after embed exists (§ Proposed behaviour Non-streaming, § Concrete)
    - **ℹ️** `end_code_block` when `has_content` false for all incoming
      - Nothing routed into GtkSource / nested
      - Close does not finalize or clear those buffers (no-op there)
      - `code_text` / notify from stored body only (§ Proposed behaviour, § Concrete)
  - **ℹ️** Streaming → Open — at `end_code_block`, `has_content` stays `true` if content remains in widgets (body not cleared) — § Proposed behaviour.
  - **ℹ️** Does not flip `false` only because the fence ended.
  - **ℹ️** Non-streaming — `.collapsed`, `.collapsed-on-done`, open.
  - **ℹ️** Streaming — `.collapsed` N/A (§ Proposed behaviour).
  - **⚠️** **Nested `MarkdownGtk.Render.is_streaming`** — **`has_content`** (same routing rule as **Incoming** → buffers / nested in § **Proposed behaviour**; **`has_widgets`** is redundant here). Update when **`has_content`** changes per matrix row — § Concrete **1c**.

- **Lazy body** (deferred GtkSource + nested markdown embed)
  - **⚠️** Ctor parses the fence and sets `this.` state needed later (including which render type).
  - **⚠️** **State flags (§ above)** — **`has_widgets`**, **`has_content`** only. **Other** instance members (e.g. `code_text`, fence chrome flags — `.collapsed`, `.collapsed-on-done` — exact names § Concrete, **private** ctor `bool` snapshot from **`Render.is_streaming`** for **fence-level** policy, field name § Concrete) are **not** additional state flags. **Nested** `MarkdownGtk.Render.is_streaming` ← **`has_content`** (§ **State flags** bullet on nested **`Render`**, § **1c**), not a parallel flag.
  - **⚠️** No **`is_streaming` / `streaming` on RSV** — § **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode** (single rule).
  - **⚠️** Exactly one new method — `private void body_widgets()` — no parameters.
    - Only creates embedded body — sets `has_widgets = true` when done — `has_content` set elsewhere (policy — feed path on/off) — § Proposed behaviour.
    - Does not resize, refill, replay, or fix layout — unless gap — plan update.
  - **⚠️** Handlers — `!has_widgets` → `body_widgets()` first where policy allows (§ Proposed behaviour per render type).
  - **ℹ️** Header / frame / revealer vs embedded bodies — § Concrete.

- **Layering**
  - **ℹ️** `Message.is_stream` stays on `OLLMchat.Message` — **`ChatView`** reads it — **`renderer.is_streaming =`** … **after** **`new MarkdownGtk.Render(box)`** (plain `bool`, no `OLLMchat` types in `libocmarkdowngtk`).
  - **⚠️** `RenderSourceView` — **state flags:** `has_widgets`, `has_content` only (§ **State flags**). **Elsewhere:** fence chrome; **private** ctor snapshot **`bool`** from **`Render.is_streaming`** for **`new RenderSourceView`** (§ **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode** — **not** a state flag). **Nested** `MarkdownGtk.Render.is_streaming` ← **`has_content`** (§ **State flags**, § **1c**).
  - **👎** Public **`is_stream`** / **`is_streaming`** / **`streaming`** on **`RenderSourceView`** — rejected (§ **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode**).

---

## User requirements

**⚠️** Authoritative.

- **⚠️** **Process — mandatory** — **Do not edit repository code** in service of this plan (no drive-by Vala, no “I already applied step 1”). Work on **this markdown file** until the user **explicitly** tells you to implement.
- **⚠️** **Accepted** — **`MarkdownGtk.Render`** — **`public bool is_streaming { get; set; }`** (no **`default =`**). **Assistant** `Render`: set **after** **`new`** by **`ChatView`** / tests (**§ 1a.2**–**1b**). **Nested** `Render` (markdown-in-fence): **`is_streaming`** from **`has_content`** in **`RenderSourceView`** — § **State flags**, § **Proposed behaviour**, § **1c** — no `OLLMchat` in `libocmarkdowngtk`.
- **⚠️** **Accepted** — **`RenderSourceView`** — third ctor parameter **`bool`** (parameter and private field names **not** specified in this plan — must **not** read as a public **`streaming`** / **`is_streaming`** API on RSV). **`Render`** passes **`this.is_streaming`**. **Private field** holds the snapshot — § Outline **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode**; § Concrete.
- **⚠️** **Rejected** — any **`is_streaming`**, **`streaming`**, or **`is_stream`** **property** (or public field) on **`RenderSourceView`** mirroring **`Message`** / **`Render`** — use **`has_widgets`** / **`has_content`** (§ **State flags**), fence chrome, and **private** ctor snapshot only (§ Outline **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode**).
- **⚠️** **Accepted** — **state flags:** `has_widgets`, `has_content` **only** (§ **State flags**). **Also:** `code_text`, fence chrome flags — § Outline — exact members § Concrete (not state flags).
- **⚠️** `OLLMchat.Message.is_stream` — message-level hint — `ChatView` / `Render` only (§ Wiring).
- **⚠️** Lazy path — omit embedded body creation in ctor when policy requires — `body_widgets()` on first need (§ Outline).

---

## 👎 Do not do / rejected

- **👎** **Touching the codebase without a direct user order** — **never** edit `Render.vala`, `RenderSourceView.vala`, `ChatView.vala`, or related sources while drafting or revising this plan. § Concrete describes **future** edits; it is **not** permission to apply them.
- **👎** Internal “pending” buffer inside `RenderSourceView` — no second `string` / `StringBuilder` / queue for chunks when `code_text` is deferred — § Outline streaming rules instead.
- **👎** Standing reconcile in `end_code_block` when not streaming — unless a real bug proves divergence (then fix cause).
- **👎** Optional product — first expand = source only — not in scope unless product asks.
- **👎** A **second** obscure stream flag on **`Render`** alongside **`is_streaming`** (e.g. duplicate “hint” APIs) — one **well-named** public **`bool`** only (§ Outline).
- **👎** Duplicating “**no streaming / `is_streaming` API on RSV**” in new bullets — § Outline **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode** is canonical; do not pad the plan with repeats.
- **👎** Extra methods on `RenderSourceView` beyond `body_widgets()` — not without updating this plan.
- **👎** Any **third** (or further) **state flag** on **`RenderSourceView`** beyond **`has_widgets`** and **`has_content`** — not approved (§ Proposed behaviour **State flags**).
- **👎** `MarkdownGtk.Render` owning `Message` — `Message.is_stream` stays in `libollmchat`.
- **👎** `accumulate_code_text_per_add` (and similar unreviewed flags) — not approved.
- **👎** `this.code_text += text` as the unconditional replacement for `code_content.append` on incremental fence path — rejected until § Concrete (§ Outline).
- **👎** Extra **`Render` / `ChatView` surface** beyond **`Render.is_streaming`** + message-boundary wiring in § Concrete — keep fence delivery minimal (§ Proposed behaviour).
- **👎** Streaming (incremental fence) + `.collapsed` render type — not in this plan — not used (§ Proposed behaviour).

## Existing indicators (**ℹ️** — tree)

- **ℹ️** `OLLMchat.Message.is_stream` — `true` for `content-stream` and `think-stream`; `false` for `content-non-stream` (`libollmchat/Message.vala`).
- **ℹ️** `ChatView` — live: `append_assistant_chunk`; history: `append_complete_assistant_message` (~559).
- **ℹ️** `MarkdownGtk.Render` — incremental `add` (`libocmarkdowngtk/Render.vala`).

---

## Placeholder vs empty `body_revealer` (**ℹ️**, **💩**)

- **ℹ️** `Gtk.Revealer.set_child` may be `null` (GTK docs).
- **ℹ️** Lazy option — no body child until `body_widgets()` / attach — then one `set_child` for the real scrolled body.
- **ℹ️** Today — `body_revealer.set_child(this.scrolled_window)` in ctor (~378–386).
- **ℹ️** Resize — `get_allocated_width()` may be 0 with no child — guard `for_width` (verify when implementing).
- **👎** Placeholder widget only to replace later — prefer null or defer attach (§ Outline).

---

## Purpose (**💩**)

- **⚠️** Reduce work during history load and restore for large fenced blocks.
- **⚠️** Defer creating GtkSource + nested markdown embed until needed (§ Outline, `body_widgets()`).
- **⚠️** Lifecycle — Streaming / Non-streaming × render types — `has_widgets` / `has_content` — § Proposed behaviour (streaming — no `.collapsed`).

---

## Scope (**💩** — table draft)

| In scope | Out of scope |
|----------|----------------|
| `RenderSourceView` — `has_widgets`, `has_content`, `code_text`, fence chrome flags, `body_widgets()`, handlers — **no** `is_streaming` / `streaming` API (§ Outline **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode**) | New public methods without approval (`CODING_STANDARDS.md`) |
| `ChatView` / `Render` — `Message.is_stream`, incremental vs single-chunk delivery — § Wiring | Parser (6.8), ProgressView / Runner (7.14.2.2) |

---

## Acceptance criteria (**💩** mixed — each ⚠️ line — confirm it matches your intent)

Flip **⚠️** → **✅** on the line when verified in tree.

- **⚠️** Lazy embed / restore — no nested `Parser.add` / GtkSource insert for the body until `body_widgets()` (or agreed min — source only).
- **⚠️** Fence delivery — incremental vs single-chunk — from `ChatView` / `Render` / `Message.is_stream` — RSV feed mode § Outline **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode** (§ Wiring).
- **⚠️** Incremental fence — `add_code_text` does not append stored body while open — § Outline.
- **⚠️** Incremental fence — stored body set at close from SourceView (and nested path as needed) — § Outline.
- **⚠️** Single-chunk / bulk — stored body accumulates per `add_code_text` like today (`code_content`) — § Outline.
- **⚠️** `end_code_block` — snapshot / `code_text` / `has_content` per § Proposed behaviour — not user revealer toggle (out of scope here).
- **⚠️** `has_widgets`, `has_content` — explicit state — not `source_view == null` alone — `has_content` = route incoming to widgets, not buffer non-emptiness (§ Proposed behaviour).
- **⚠️** Handlers that need the body — ensure `body_widgets()` has run before touching GtkSource / nested render — § Outline.

---

## Current behaviour (reference)

- **ℹ️** Ctor builds GtkSource, stack, nested `MarkdownGtk.Render` eagerly.
- **ℹ️** `add_code_text` — `code_content`, `source_buffer`, `nested_markdown_render.add` (~573–595).
- **ℹ️** Collapse hides body — does not clear buffers today.

---

## Wiring (**ℹ️** — `libocmarkdowngtk` stays `OLLMchat`-free)

- **ℹ️** **`ChatView`** sets **`renderer.is_streaming`** from **`Message.is_stream`** (or **`false`** for complete-message / history paths) **before** `add()` for that assistant message — supports **load + continue**: same `Render`, **flip** when switching from replay to live stream (§ Outline **`Render.is_streaming`**).
- **ℹ️** RSV **fence** feed-mode — § Outline **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode** (ctor third arg from **`Render.is_streaming`** at **`on_code_block`**).
- **ℹ️** **Nested** `MarkdownGtk.Render` — **`is_streaming`** from **`has_content`** (§ **State flags**, § **Proposed behaviour**, § **1c**).
- **ℹ️** `libocmarkdowngtk` must not depend on `OLLMchat`.
- **⚠️** `append_assistant_chunk` vs `append_complete_assistant_message` — different feed shapes; **`ChatView`** must set **`renderer.is_streaming`** accordingly **each** message (§ User requirements).
- **ℹ️** `RenderSourceView` — **`has_widgets`**, **`has_content`** (§ **State flags**), fence chrome, ctor snapshot **`bool`** for **`new RenderSourceView`**, `add_code_text` / `end_code_block` — no `Message` on RSV (§ Outline **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode**).

---

## Proposed behaviour (contract) (**💩** — whole section is assistant matrix unless you promoted it; cross-check § User requirements + 👎)

**⚠️** **Matrix scope:** **Streaming** = parser-driven fence / incremental `add_code_text` (§ Outline **How fences get created**). **Non-streaming** = bulk / history / restore where behaviour differs (**not** the same incremental fence discovery path for this plan’s targets).

**💩** — **ℹ️** Matrix (assistant)
- Two axes: fence mode (Streaming vs Non-streaming) × render type
- Track `has_widgets` and `has_content` at ctor / incoming / close
- Close means `end_code_block` only
- Not user toggling revealer / expand / collapse (out of scope here)

**💩** — **ℹ️** Streaming + `.collapsed` (body hidden at start)
- **NOT SUPPORTED** — **NOT USED**
- Live incremental fences use `.collapsed-on-done` (`libollmchatgtk/ChatView.vala`, `examples/oc-test-gtkmd.vala`)
- Do not plan or implement streaming + `.collapsed`

**💩** — **ℹ️** Flag meanings
- `has_widgets` — embedded body built (`body_widgets()` finished)
- `has_content` — incoming stream should write to the widgets (buffers / nested render)
- Routing policy
- Not “is there text in the buffer yet”

**💩** — **ℹ️** `code_text`
- Persisted string
- Updated at `end_code_block` / fence finished (§ Concrete)

**💩** — **ℹ️** Single-chunk fence
- Treat as Non-streaming in this matrix even if message streams (§ Outline)

- **Streaming** (incremental `add_code_text` — `.collapsed-on-done` or Open)
  - **Render type — Collapsed** (`.collapsed`)
    - **NOT SUPPORTED** — **NOT USED** — no ctor / incoming / close matrix — `ChatView` thinking uses `collapsed-on-done`, not `.collapsed`.

  - **Render type — Collapse at end** (`.collapsed-on-done` — chrome name from tree; behaviour here only ctor / incoming / `end_code_block`)
    - **💩** **Ctor** — streaming collapse-at-end
      - **ℹ️** Assistant detail draft — confirm before build
      - **Parse**
        - Opening fence and attributes
        - Record collapse-at-end from chrome
        - Names: `.collapsed-on-done` / `collapse_on_done` (exact identifiers § Concrete)
        - Meaning: body hidden until fence closes (revealer / product)
        - Not the same as `has_widgets` false
      - `body_widgets()`
        - Call in ctor or immediately after parse
        - Required on this matrix row
        - GtkSource + stack + nested `MarkdownGtk.Render` before first `add_code_text`
        - Body may still be visually collapsed
        - On success: `has_widgets` = true
      - `has_content`
        - Set by routing policy, not by ctor completion alone
        - Usually `has_content` = true before first `add_code_text`
        - Chunks go to buffers / nested, not `+=` stored per chunk (§ 👎)
        - Flag means route incoming here
        - Does not mean buffer already non-empty
        - Does not mean revealer is expanded
      - **Guard**
        - If `body_widgets()` never runs, `has_widgets` stays false
        - Should not happen on this row
        - Handlers § Outline
    - **Incoming**
      - When `has_content`: `add_code_text` writes buffers / nested — `has_widgets` = true
      - Do not `+=` stored string per chunk on incremental path (§ 👎)
    - **Close** (`end_code_block`)
      - Finalize `code_text` from SourceView / nested (§ Concrete)
      - `has_content` = false
      - Fence ended
      - No further `add_code_text` for this block (stop routing incoming to widgets)
      - Clear buffers / flush nested per tree (§ Concrete)

  - **Render type — Open** (body visible from start — LLM content as it runs)
    - **Ctor**
      - `body_widgets()` in ctor or immediately after parse — `has_widgets` = true before first incoming
      - `has_content` = true before first incoming
      - Incoming stream will write into widgets (same routing flag as during the fence)
    - **Incoming**
      - `has_widgets` = true; `has_content` = true while `add_code_text` feeds the fence
    - **Close** (`end_code_block`)
      - Write `code_text` from buffers (§ Concrete)
      - Do not clear visible body
      - Content stays on screen
      - `has_content` = true (content still in widgets; not `false` just because the fence closed) — § Concrete

- **Non-streaming** (single-chunk, history, restore — `.collapsed` allowed)
  - **Render type — Collapsed**
    - **Ctor**
      - `has_widgets` = false, `has_content` = false — parse only (§ Outline)
    - **Incoming**
      - Often one `add_code_text`
      - `has_widgets` false → defer `body_widgets()` until policy is fixed
      - After `body_widgets()` (`has_widgets` true), `has_content` selects routing
      - `+=` stored body when policy keeps buffers off-tree `has_content` false (§ Concrete)
    - **Close** (`end_code_block`)
      - `code_text` matches accumulated incoming; notify (§ Outline)
      - If `has_content` was false throughout (nothing routed to widgets), widget finalize / clear are no-ops
      - Only stored string mattered (§ Concrete)

  - **Render type — Collapse at end**
    - **💩** **Ctor** — non-streaming collapse-at-end
      - **ℹ️** Assistant detail draft — confirm before build
      - **Parse**
        - Opening fence and attributes
        - Record collapse-at-end from chrome (`.collapsed-on-done`, § Concrete / tree)
        - Restore / history: block was hidden until done
        - Distinct from non-streaming `.collapsed` only (§ Proposed behaviour)
      - **Chrome without embed**
        - Frame, header, revealer as today when embed not required yet
        - § Placeholder vs `body_revealer`
      - **Lazy embed** (default when policy allows)
        - Do not call `body_widgets()` in ctor
        - After parse: `has_widgets` = false
        - After parse: `has_content` = false
        - No GtkSource / stack / nested markdown yet
        - Incoming cannot route into widgets that do not exist
        - Single-chunk may only `+=` stored body until `body_widgets()` (§ Outline)
      - **Eager embed** (policy requires embed before `add_code_text`)
        - Call `body_widgets()` in ctor or right after parse
        - On return: `has_widgets` = true
        - `has_content` still from policy alone
        - `has_content` = true only when incoming should write GtkSource / nested
        - `has_content` = false when incoming should only `+=` stored string
        - Example: buffers off-tree until expand or `end_code_block` (§ Concrete)
        - `body_widgets()` does not imply `has_content` = true (§ Outline)
      - **Revealer vs `has_widgets`**
        - Visually collapsed body does not imply `has_widgets` false
        - User may not see scrolled body yet
        - `has_widgets`: whether embed exists in widget tree
    - **Incoming**
      - `+=` stored body each `add_code_text`
      - Buffers when `has_widgets` and `has_content`
      - `has_content` — `true` when policy routes incoming into widgets (independent of how many characters so far)
    - **Close**
      - Stored body complete — notify
      - `has_content` = false at `end_code_block`
      - `code_text` holds snapshot (§ Concrete)

  - **Render type — Open**
    - **Ctor**
      - `body_widgets()` early — `has_widgets` = true
    - **Incoming**
      - `+=` stored body
      - Buffers in sync
    - **Close**
      - Notify
      - May persist `code_text` without clearing buffers (same idea as streaming Open)

- **Shared** (all modes / types)
  - `body_widgets()` — private, no parameters — sets `has_widgets` = true — § Outline
  - Handlers — `!has_widgets` where embed required — `body_widgets()` first (§ Outline)
  - **💩** — **ℹ️** `end_code_block`
    - Widget snapshot / clear steps apply only where content went through `has_content` routing into widgets
    - Otherwise close is stored-string / notify only (§ Outline state flags)

---

## Concrete code proposals

**🚫** Fenced Vala below is **spec only**. **Do not** apply patches to the repo from this section unless the user explicitly asks for implementation. Same as **Status** banner and § User requirements (process).

**ℹ️** Order
- Do one numbered block at a time
- Each block ends with Keep / Remove / Replace with / Add + fenced Vala per `docs/guide-to-writing-plans.md` before starting the next
- Avoids the last mixed diff

### 1. Call sites — `new MarkdownGtk.Render` (tree)

**ℹ️** **`Render`** stays **`Render(Gtk.Box box)`** only — **no** second ctor argument (§ **`libocmarkdowngtk/Render.vala`** — **`Add`** **`is_streaming`** property only).

**⚠️** **Outer `Render` / fence** — **`Render.on_code_block` → `new RenderSourceView(..., this.is_streaming)`** reads **`Render.is_streaming` at fence open.** Set **`renderer.is_streaming`** **before `add()`** for that assistant message (**§ 1a.2**, **§ 1a.3**, **§ 1b**). **⚠️** **Nested `MarkdownGtk.Render`** — **`nested_markdown_render.is_streaming`** from **`has_content`** (§ **State flags**, § **Proposed behaviour**) **before** nested **`start()`** (**§ 1c**). If **`has_*` routing** does not match the matrix, **nothing in this plan’s matrix applies**.

**💩** **`public bool is_streaming { get; set; default = false; }`** — **Rejected** — redundant **`default =`**; value comes from **explicit** assignments (§ Outline).

### 1a. `libollmchatgtk/ChatView.vala` — assistant `Render`

**ℹ️** **No** **`Remove` / `Replace with`** — tree already has **`new MarkdownGtk.Render(this.text_view_box) { … }`**. Feed mode is **§ 1a.2** / **1a.3** only.

### 1a.2 `libollmchatgtk/ChatView.vala` — **`initialize_assistant_message`** (live / streaming chunks)

**Why:** First line that puts **`Message.is_stream`** into **`Render`** for the current assistant round — **before** any **`process_new_chunk` → `add()`**.

#### Keep (anchor — last lines before insert)

```vala
			this.content_state = ContentState.NONE;
			// Initialize the renderer for the new assistant message
			this.renderer.start();
```

#### Add

```vala
			this.renderer.is_streaming = response.message.is_stream;
```

### 1a.3 `libollmchatgtk/ChatView.vala` — **`append_complete_assistant_message`** (history / complete body)

**Why:** Same field for **replay** — typically **`message.is_stream` false** for stored bulk; **`true`** if persisted stream semantics matter.

#### Keep

```vala
			this.is_thinking = is_thinking;
```

#### Add

```vala
			this.renderer.is_streaming = message.is_stream;
```

*(Insert **Add** immediately after **Keep**; existing comments and **`// Process thinking …`** block follow unchanged.)*

### 1b. `examples/oc-test-gtkmd.vala` — demo `Render`

**Why:** **`stream`** is **`(opt_stream_delay_sec >= 0)`** — assign **`md_renderer.is_streaming`** after construction so simulated chunked feed matches. **ℹ️** If **JSON history** replay should always be bulk-shaped, set **`md_renderer.is_streaming = false`** before **`render_history()`** — product rule.

#### Keep (anchor)

```vala
		this.md_renderer = new MarkdownGtk.Render(this.text_view_box) {
			scroll_to_end = this.history_messages.get_length() == 0 && stream
		};
```

#### Add (immediately after **Keep**)

```vala
		this.md_renderer.is_streaming = stream;
```

### 1c. `libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala` — nested `Render` (markdown code blocks)

**Why:** § **State flags** — **`has_content`** selects routing into GtkSource / **nested** render (widgets are already required for that path). **`nested_markdown_render.is_streaming = has_content`** — § **Proposed behaviour** matrix (**Incoming** / **Lazy embed** rows). Update when **`has_content`** changes.

#### Remove

```vala
				this.nested_markdown_render = new MarkdownGtk.Render(this.rendered_box);
				this.nested_markdown_render.start();
```

#### Replace with

```vala
				this.nested_markdown_render = new MarkdownGtk.Render(this.rendered_box);
				this.nested_markdown_render.is_streaming = this.has_content;
				this.nested_markdown_render.start();
```

**ℹ️** If **`body_widgets()`** creates nested **before** **`has_content`** is final for that phase, set **`is_streaming`** again when **`has_content`** matches § **Proposed behaviour** for that row.

---

### 2. Call sites — `new RenderSourceView` (tree)

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `libocmarkdowngtk/Render.vala` | **`new RenderSourceView(this, lang, this.is_streaming)`** inside `on_code_block` — snapshot **Render** feed mode (§ Outline **fundamental**) |
| `libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala` | Ctor gains third `bool` (parameter name § Concrete) + behaviour |
| `libollmchatgtk/ChatView.vala` | No `new RenderSourceView` — **`renderer.is_streaming`** from **`Message.is_stream`** in **`initialize_assistant_message`** / **`append_complete_assistant_message`** (§ **1a.2**, **1a.3**); **`thinking_frame = renderer.childview`** after manual `on_code_block` for thinking |

**Ctor third arg** — **`bool`** ← **`Render.is_streaming`** at fence open — must be set **before `add()`** for that message (§ **1** **⚠️**, § **1a.2**, **1a.3**).

---

### `libocmarkdowngtk/Render.vala`

**Why:** **`public bool is_streaming`** — assistant **`Render`:** set **after** **`new`** (**`ChatView`** / tests — **§ 1a.2**–**1b**), read at **`on_code_block`** for **`new RenderSourceView`**. **Nested** **`Render`:** **`is_streaming`** from **`has_content`** (**§ 1c**, § **State flags**).

#### Add (property — outline, no `default`)

```vala
public bool is_streaming { get; set; }
```

*(Docblock: set by app **after** construction, before **`add()`** for each assistant feed; read at **`on_code_block`**.)*

**ℹ️** If Vala insists on an initializer for the auto-property, use a **private backing field** — avoid **`default =`** on the public contract (implementation detail).

#### Remove

```vala
this.childview = new RenderSourceView(this, lang);
```

#### Replace with

```vala
this.childview = new RenderSourceView(this, lang, this.is_streaming);
```

**Why:** Third argument snapshots **`Render.is_streaming`** at fence open.

**💩** **Assistant draft** — **`this.is_streaming = false`** in **`clear()`**. **Rejected** — not logical here; **`is_streaming`** is feed behaviour, not cleared with the view (§ Outline).

---

### `libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala`

**Why:** Lazy / `has_widgets` / `has_content` / `body_widgets()` — § Concrete steps **3–6**; ctor gains third **`bool`** (parameter + private field names § Concrete; **not** a state flag — § **State flags**).

#### Add (ctor signature — outline)

*(Third `bool` argument: snapshot of `Render.is_streaming` at fence open. Parameter and field names **not** fixed in this plan.)*

```vala
public RenderSourceView(Render renderer, string language_id, bool /* … */)
```

*(Placeholder — not valid Vala. Exact signature in step 4 — ctor break-up — when approved.)*

### `libollmchatgtk/ChatView.vala` (message-boundary assignments — see § **1a.2**, **1a.3**)

**Why:** **`ChatView`** injects **`Message.is_stream`** into **`renderer.is_streaming`** before **`add()`** — **verbatim fences in § 1a.2 / 1a.3**; do not duplicate here.

- **💩** **Assistant-only optional** — **`this.renderer.is_streaming = false`** in **`finalize_assistant_message`** — **not** requested; **`clear()`** does **not** reset **`is_streaming`** (§ Outline, § `Render.vala` above).

### 3. New state on `RenderSourceView`
- **State flags (only):** `has_widgets`, `has_content` (§ Outline **State flags** — nothing else).
- **Other members:** `code_text`, fence chrome flags (§ Outline).
- Initial values follow § Proposed behaviour (streaming matrix for parser path; non-streaming for bulk load path — § Proposed behaviour **Matrix scope**)

### 4. Ctor break-up — split current `RenderSourceView` ctor
- Parse and record chrome and language
- Set `this.` flags from § Proposed behaviour criteria
- Optional lazy path: skip embedded body
- Header, frame, revealer without embed where allowed (§ Placeholder vs `body_revealer`)
- Then call `body_widgets()` only where policy requires eager embed

### 5. `body_widgets()`
- Extract existing GtkSource + stack + nested `MarkdownGtk.Render` construction into one `private void body_widgets()`
- End with `has_widgets` = true
- No `has_content` assignment inside (§ Outline)

### 6. Handlers / feed paths
- Update `add_code_text`, `end_code_block`, and any helper that writes GtkSource / nested render / `code_text`
- `!has_widgets` → `body_widgets()` first where policy allows
- Gate buffer writes on `has_content`
- If **`nested_markdown_render` != null**, keep **`nested_markdown_render.is_streaming`** aligned with **`has_content`** whenever routing changes (§ **State flags**, § **1c**)
- `end_code_block` snapshot / clear / notify per § Proposed behaviour and § Outline
- Include no-op close when `has_content` never routed

### 7. `libocmarkdown` / other renderers
- **No** `on_code_block` signature change required for this plan’s stream policy (§ Outline **fundamental**)
- `examples/oc-test-gtkmd.vala`: **`md_renderer.is_streaming = stream`** after **`new MarkdownGtk.Render`** (§ **1b**); history replay may need **`md_renderer.is_streaming = false`** before **`render_history`** if CLI **`stream`** is true — § **1** **⚠️** / **1b** **Why**.

- **ℹ️** Prior verbatim Vala drafts were removed — they contradicted `has_widgets` / `has_content` (§ Proposed behaviour).
- **ℹ️** Each step above gets its own Keep / Remove / Replace with / Add subsection + fenced Vala when you approve that step — `docs/guide-to-writing-plans.md` — Code proposals section.

- **ℹ️** `oc-test-gtkmd` `--thinking` / `--stream` — no duplicate widgets.
